characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Abhorrent Admirer: To any unfortunate woman who catches his eye. Alliterative Name:RichardRichard. All of the Other Reindeer: As demonstrated in many episodes, but particularly in the episode "Parade". Ambiguously Bi: This characterization shows up mainly in the live shows, where Richie's desperation to get laid sometimes caused him to hit on Eddie, but there were a couple of hints in the TV series as well, such as his excitement at the prospect of seeing a nude photo of Desmond Lynam and his admission to appreciating a man's pectorals, even after finding it out he wasn't a woman like he initially believed. There’s also his very feminine personality. Attention Whore: He's desperately, pathetically needy for companionship and attention, leading to most of his flouncing, idiotic plans and tantrums. On one occasion, he tries to fake his own attempted suicide in order to try and get Eddie to buy him a drink.

characters.name: Richard "Richie" Richard
characters.description: Abhorrent Admirer: To any unfortunate woman who catches his eye. Alliterative Name:RichardRichard. All of the Other Reindeer: As demonstrated in many episodes, but particularly in the episode "Parade". Ambiguously Bi: This characterization shows up mainly in the live shows, where Richie's desperation to get laid sometimes caused him to hit on Eddie, but there were a couple of hints in the TV series as well, such as his excitement at the prospect of seeing a nude photo of Desmond Lynam and his admission to appreciating a man's pectorals, even after finding it out he wasn't a woman like he initially believed. There’s also his very feminine personality. Attention Whore: He's desperately, pathetically needy for companionship and attention, leading to most of his flouncing, idiotic plans and tantrums. On one occasion, he tries to fake his own attempted suicide in order to try and get Eddie to buy him a drink.

characters.name: Edward "Eddie" Elizabeth Hitler
characters.description: Alcohol Hic: OneRunning Gag, when someone notices that some alcohol has gone missing, Eddie will randomly hiccup. The Alcoholic: He's a cheerfully violent dipsomaniac who will drink absolutely anything, to the point where he's right up there withFather Jackin the world of sitcom alcoholics.Richie:[after seeing Eddie drink a cocktail consisting of brandy, meths, Pernod, paint stripper, Mr Sheen, brake fluid and Drambuie]How are you still alive?Eddie: I may very well not be. Ambiguously Bi: Although not as much as Richie, he smiles and agrees with Richie's romantic fantasizing about Ted Rogers in "Finger." There's also theNoodle IncidentwithTony Blair. In the second live show, he's named his sex doll Les Ferdinand after the real-life football player, thenhastily claims that Les is short for Lesbo. Aside Glance: He has a habit of looking directly and increduously at the camera whenever Richie says something overly ridiculous. Becoming the Mask: In "Finger", he gets a little too into the role of "Edina", to the point of openly flirting with a barman.

characters.name: Spudgun and Dave Hedgehog
characters.description: Big Guy, Little Guy: Spudgun is the big guy, Dave Hedgehog the little. Characterization Marches On: In their first episode, Dave Hedgehog's the comparatively saner of the two, while Spudgun's even more simple-minded and alcoholic than in his later appearances. However, Hedgehog'sComically Missing the Pointmoment when answering Eddie's question about what medieval people did before the invention of TV got such a huge laugh from the studio audience that hisCloud Cuckoo Landeraspects were heavily played up in all his remaining appearances. Cloud Cuckoo Lander: They're both pretty eccentric, but Dave Hedgehog takes the cake. Deadpan Snarker: Their usual attempts at humour, especially Spudgun's. Early-Installment Weirdness: In the duo's first appearance, it was implied that "Hedgehog" was just a nickname Dave had. But in "Terror", his daughter Doreen states it as their actual last name.

characters.name: Dick Head
characters.description: Manipulative Bastard: Often manages to turn Richie and Eddie's scams back on them. For instance, after they try to pay him with Eddie's pornographic forged money, he rats them out to "Skullcrusher" Henderson, then in turn dupes them into paying £200 each to enter that night's pub quiz, whose prize happens to be exactly the amount they need to come up with to avoid a very nasty fate — but also happens to be made up of Skullcrusher's own forged cash, with is useless for paying off their debt. Punny Name: Hehe, get it? Dick Head?

characters.name: Mr. Harrison
characters.description: Child Hater: His attitude towards his infant grandson inHoly. Hates Their Parent: This is obvious given how he talks about his deceased mother inBottom's Up. Jerkass: He generally doesn't seem to like anyone, not even his own family members.

characters.name: Mr Rottweiler
characters.description: Berserk Button: The mere presence of Richie and Eddie is this for him. He grabs them by their ties and nearly chokes them when they try to call round. This is especially the case when he finds out through the gas-man that they were trespassing in his flat. He promptly smashes through the wall and throttles them. Big "SHUT UP!": "SHUT IT! Or I'll start shooting" in a deleted scene. Big "WHAT?!": When he shuts Richie's fingers in the door. Blatant Lies: Richie describes him as "a nice-enough man", he's anything but... Breaching the Wall: Played with, since Richie and Eddie broke down his bedroom wall in the first place to get into his flat and sabotage his gas supply. He breaks through the wall and throttles them at the end of the episode after finding out about their trespassing from the gas-man.

characters.name: Veronica Head
characters.description: Jerkass: She takes after her Uncle in this respect. Manipulative Bitch: She turns out to be in on her Uncle and Larry's scheme to con Richie, Eddie and all their friends so that they all end up paying for their drinks anyway.

characters.name: Doreen Hedgehog
characters.description: Women Are Wiser: Downplayed, since most of themenon the show are also wiser than her father, but it's clear that she and her mother are the ones with the smarts in the Hedgehog family, with Doreen being extremely bemused by Richie's antics in trying to make aDeal with the Devilwith her, and off-handedly mentioning that her mother doesn't like Dave hanging around with Richie, Eddie and Spudgun after midnight.

characters.name: Lady Natasha Letitia Sarah Jane Wellesley Obstromsky Ponsonsky Smythe Smythe Smythe Smythe Smythe Oblomov Boblomov Dob, third Viscountess of Moldavi
characters.description: Gold Digger: Her family have become impoverished so she only intends to marry Richie for his (non-existent) money.

characters.name: "Skullcrusher" Henderson
characters.description: Counterfeit Cash: Despite his supposedly being a master forger, his forgeries are barely any better than Eddie's, featuring drag artist DannyLaRueinstead of the Queen. It's implied that he became London's top forger just by crushing the skulls of any rivals, and presumably anyone who refused to accept his forgeries as legal tender. Disproportionate Retribution: Richie and Eddie use their forged cash to swindle Dick Head out of maybe £10 worth of drinks, so Skullcrusher demands a£5,000tribute on pain of having their skulls crushed if they fail to pay up. Though this can be somewhat justified in that he didn't know how much of their forged cash was in circulation, and that they just had the bad luck to have the first person they tried palming it off to be Dick, who happened to be friends with Skullcrusher. Names to Run Away from Really Fast: There's averygood reason why he picked up the nickname "Skullcrusher", which Richie and Eddie find out the hard way after unwittingly trying to pay him off with his own forged cash.

characters.name: Mr Tent
characters.description: Groin Attack: He gets his genitals caught in the zipper of Richie and Eddie's tent. Teeny Weenie: Even smaller than Richie's, apparently.

characters.name: "Cannonball" Taffy O'Jones
characters.description: Ambiguously Bi: It's mentioned that there were some steamy goings-on in the cricket pavillion's changing room brought on by the sight of the unconscious Richie, who Taffy had just knocked out with a cricket ball straight to the head. Taffy's being part of these goings-on allowed Eddie to lift his car keys. Odd Friendship: It's only alluded to, but he's apparently friends with Spudgun, who's the captain of their cricket team. Small Role, Big Impact: He's the last character other than Richie or Eddie to have any lines in the series, as the following series finale is aBottle Episodethat only stars the two main characters. In terms of the episode, he only shows up at the very end and doesn't even appear on-screen, but his knocking Richie senseless with a cricket ball to the head gets the episode's storyline going. The Voice: He's never actually seen on-screen, only being described as a "big seven foot neanderthal figure" with a bride who looks like Ted Rogers. Keeping him off-screen also had the advantage of allowing Kevin Allen, who plays Marcel the waiter (and ironically was the person to describe him as such) to double up as Taffy's voice.
